scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from when the loan is made. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are known to each other and the Defendant approached the Plaintiff for financial assistance in the form of a hand loan.

6. On 15.03.2024, acting on the request of the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) towards the said loan. The aforementioned amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by the bank transaction record marked as Annexure A.

7. Following the transfer of funds, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages communicated on {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATES}}, copies of which are preserved and marked as Annexure B.

8. It was mutually understood between the parties that the loan amount was payable on demand, and the Defendant was obligated to repay the principal sum along with agreed interest upon the Plaintiff's request.

BREACH OF OBLIGATION

9. Despite the mutual understanding and the Plaintiff's repeated oral demands for repayment, the Defendant failed to discharge the liability within a reasonable time.

10. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues, a copy of which is marked as Annexure C. The Defendant received the said notice but did not comply with the same.

11. The Defendant has failed to repay the loan amount despite the expiry of the notice period and the date of default being fixed at 20.06.2024, thereby committing a breach of the obligation to repay the hand loan advanced by the Plaintiff.

DAMAGES

12. By reason of the matters aforesaid, the Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- from the Defendant, which remains outstanding and due as on the date of filing this plaint.

13. The Plaintiff is further entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation, quantifying the financial loss suffered by the Plaintiff due to the wrongful withholding of the money by the Defendant.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

15. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

17. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

18. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Proced
